{{Quote|I am but a leaf blown by the winds of the Force. I trust my purpose in being anywhere will eventually be revealed to me.|Zao|<ref name="R33" />}}
'''Zao''' was a blind, elderly [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Veknoid/Legends|Veknoid]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]], and an accomplished [[Chef/Legends|chef]], who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the final [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. He did not accept missions from the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]], but followed the will of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] as he traveled about [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Those who ate Zao's cooking often found that its taste, whether good or bad, reflected the degree to which their current mental state was in harmony with the Force. In spite of his blindness, he remained a formidable and lethal adversary, wielding his unique [[long-handle lightsaber]] with frightening accuracy.

==Biography==
===A wandering Jedi===
[[File:Zaocane3.jpg|thumb|left|140px|Master Zao]]
{{Quote|The war is already won or lost as the Force wills. We are all leaves blown by its wind.|Zao|<ref name="R73">[[Republic 73|''Republic'' 73]]</ref>}}
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Veknoid/Legends|Veknoid]], Zao was [[Jedi training/Legends|educated]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], attaining the rank of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]], by which time he was blind. By the time the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] began to grow more potent, Master Zao no longer took on formal missions for the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]]. Instead, the Veknoid Jedi traveled as he believed the Force directed him, moving from one [[Planet/Legends|world]] to another as the currents of the Force flowed. At some point, Zao constructed a [[Color/Legends|blue]]-bladed [[Sabercane/Legends|lightsaber cane]], using it as a walking stick, weapon, and cooking instrument.<ref name="R33" />

In [[30 BBY/Legends|30 BBY]], Zao was serving as a [[Chef/Legends|chef]] to the [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Gorto Zaga]] on [[Kiffex/Legends|Kiffex]].<ref name="R33" /> Through his cooking, Zao attempted to teach his philosophy to those who consumed it: whether the food tasted bad or good was determined by the balance within the consumer. Those who were not in balance with the Force would find the food foul or bitter; those with good balance would enjoy the meal. As Zaga was an immoral criminal, he found Zao's cooking to be unpleasant.<ref name="R73" />

Departing the crime lord's company, Zao stumbled upon [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]], who was in search of his missing [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]]. Believing the Force had brought them together for this very purpose, Zao aided Vos and Master [[Tholme/Legends|Tholme]] in fighting off Zaga's guards. Soon, however, the planet's feral population of [[Anzat (species)|Anzati]] stormed the [[Black Hole (Kiffex)|Black Hole]] [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]]. Discovering that Secura was leading the feral Anzati, Vos and Tholme set out to free her from the thrall of the dark side of the Force; Zao took his leave of the other Jedi and departed the world.<ref name="SW34">[[Star Wars (1998) 34|''Star Wars'' (1998) 34]]</ref>

===Clone Wars===
[[File:Zao_survivor.jpg|thumb|150px|Zao eludes Order 66]]
When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] erupted a decade later between the Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Zao invoked the [[Right of Denial]] and did not take up his lightsaber in the fight; the Veknoid believed the war was in direct violation of the will of the Force. Instead, Zao continued wandering between the stars; the High Council so seldom heard from Zao that many believed him [[Death/Legends|dead]]. By the [[19 BBY/Legends|last year]] of the war, Zao had set up a food-selling hovel on the streets of [[Saleucami/Legends|Saleucami's]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]].<ref name="R73" />

During his stay on Saleucami, Master Tholme came across the aging Veknoid's food stand and took a meal with his old colleague. Investigating rumors that [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Sora Bulq/Legends|Sora Bulq]] was amassing a [[Morgukai Shadow Army|large army]] of [[Cloning/Legends|cloned]] [[Morgukai]] warriors, Tholme asked for Zao's assistance. Despite Zao believing that the Order was misguided in serving a corrupt Republic, the old Master agreed to point Tholme toward the hidden entrance to Bulq's underground cloning facilities.<ref name="R73" />

===Great Jedi Purge===
Several weeks after his encounter with Tholme, the Republic and the Jedi Order were destroyed by the machinations of the Republic's [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]], who was in fact the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]]. Despite the lingering presence of members of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] on Saleucami and their new [[Order 66/Legends|standing orders]] to execute all members of the Jedi, Zao was able to avoid detection. Dressed as a beggar, Zao's anonymity ensured his safety during this time.<ref name="R79">[[Republic 79|''Republic'' 79]]</ref>

Having survived the early stages of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]], Zao eluded detection until his meeting in a refugee camp on [[Arkinnea]] with Jedi Master [[K'Kruhk]], a Padawan named [[Chase Piru]] and nine [[Youngling/Legends|younglings]] under the Whiphid's protection. After briefly mentoring the younglings, Zao confided in K'Kruhk that he sensed great danger in the camp and suggested they leave immediately. The Jedi escaped the encampment. K'Kruhk then stole a wagon while Zao led the younglings to gather food from the farmlands. But as they made their way into the mountains, they discovered that the [[Militia/Legends|militia]] had massacred the refugees and dumped their bodies there. They were soon spotted by the militia. K'Kruhk tried to draw them away while Zao escorted the younglings to safety, but the militia captain [[Relik (individual)|Relik]] sent a group to kill them. While fleeing, Zao and the others encountered the native [[Yunu]], who were also subject to the militia's attacks. Zao defended them all by pulling a boulder out of the ground. He told the younglings to flee, but one of them, [[Sidirri]], pushed the boulder onto the militia in anger, killing them.<ref name="FireCarrier" />

The Yunu brought the Jedi to an [[Hidden Temple (Arkinnea)|abandoned Jedi outpost]], and soon K'Kruhk also joined them. There the Jedi were able to hide from the Empire for several years. Sidirri eventually ran away, and Zao left to find her. His fate is unknown.<ref name="FireCarrier" />

==Powers and abilities==
Zao was a powerful yet mysterious Jedi who relied on the Force to control almost all natures of his life due to his blindness. He was skilled with his lightsaber staff, despite his blindness. He also created a soup that, according to him, was completely in harmony with the Force: those who tasted it experienced different flavors depending on their own balance within the Force. Tholme and [[T'ra Saa/Legends|T'ra Saa]], for example, thought it was delicious, while Quinlan Vos tasted nothing, and [[Vilmarh Grahrk]] found it disgusting.<ref name="R33" />

In combat Zao could deflect a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] bolt from an [[Arkinnea Militia]] soldier, and [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinetically]] "squeeze" a rock formation out of the ground to act as a shield.<ref name="FireCarrier" /> He was also a skilled [[Telepathy/Legends|telepath]], as he aided Tholme and T'ra Saa in their efforts to help Quinlan Vos remain in the light.<ref name="R35">[[Star Wars (1998) 35|''Star Wars'' (1998) 35]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Zao's name was adapted from [[Wikipedia:Tao|Tao]], the Chinese philosophy. The way of Tao is very similar to Zao's beliefs.<ref name="JCF1">{{JCFCite|url=posts/21677884|board=Authors and Artists|thread=The Official John Ostrander Discussion Thread|user=jfostrander|date=2005-09-23|time=7:52 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160810072346/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-john-ostrander-discussion-thread.5863919/page-78|quote=I was thinking of killing her off early, just to up Quin's angst quotient, but Jan's daughter Sian asked us not to. I'm glad she did!}}</ref>

Zao was originally going to die in ''Darkness'', but according to artist [[Jan Duursema]], "Zao would have been toast in Darkness, but we were asked not to kill him."<ref name="JCF2">{{JCFCite|url=posts/23506828|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #1 (pre-release discussion)|user=Jan_Duursema|date=2006-03-25|time=7:36 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801161256/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/legacy-1-pre-release-discussion.23358131/page-15}}</ref>
